title: cyrtoceras lindleyanum
intro: Cyrtoceras lindleyanum is a type of ancient plant, specifically a fossilized species of ammonite, which is an extinct genus of shelled cephalopod mollusks that lived during the Jurassic period. The medicinal properties of Cyrtoceras lindleyanum are largely based on its fossilized shells, which are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with this plant are said to include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of allergies. Herbal preparations of Cyrtoceras lindleyanum include the use of its fossilized shells in teas, capsules, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
